title: Sarcopodium triflorum

The genus Sarcopodium is part of the subtribe Orchidinae. The classification of this orchid species is based on its floral morphology and anatomical characteristics. Further study is needed to confirm its exact classification and relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Sarcopodium triflorum, also known as Indian gentian, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as digestive problems, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Sarcopodium triflorum include gentiopicrin, gentiogenin, and loganin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sarcopodium triflorum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
